Meeting will focus on Zycus differentiation and platform Demo. 23rd April - I have shared them the Zycus differentiation for Bandhan bankbased on their pain points. 17th April - We had a joint meeting with their entire team from payment, procurement and supplier team - this was information gathering call. We captured their pain point. Meeting scheduled for 16th mar-2.30 pm proposal queries discussion. MEETING DONE - Email with collaterals shared to review our offering. 27th Feb'26 - Spoke to Mrinmoy over Indian number - He said they are evaluating and assesing the proposals. Will take another week and he will revert he said. 24th Feb'25 - Spoke to Saipayan - He said its still under technical evaluation with mrinmoy yet - check with him 17th Feb'26 - Spoke to Mrinmoy Mahapatra from Bandhan Bank - And told that we have filled the responses to the RFP shared by them. and as clarified them to ask if they have any further queries about it and told we have strong intent to be taking this ahead and getting shortlisted. 16th Feb'26 - Spoke to Saipayan from Bandhan - He clearly stated that they cant extend the further date of response submission to RFP + he stated the repsonse submission doesnt have a fixed format - we can submit in our format or template catering to the requirements and asks mentioned in the RFP ( techno-functional ask in RFP) - He said that needs to be sent to the person mentioned in the rfp - who will further evaluate all vendors and their responses and then share it with procrement team - then the commercials are brought to picture and collected - then they will be rating the Vendors L1,L2 etc and then shortlist them based on the highest feasible -- May ask for demo between these stages after technical evalution or even after the commercial evalution depends on the team. Then will go submit and finalize the bid. This is what he said. Now - he aked us to submit the response by 17th EOD - no extension and submit in our format. 11th Feb'26 - RFP Response date extended to 17th Feb email received. -- Shared this rfp and extension internally to Carl and Akshay to guide next steps. 5th Feb 2026| Ria - RFP RECEIVED 10% confidence 12th Feb 2026- Prospect has come back with a "late to Zycus" RFP- suspiciously looks like we are being added as a column B in any evaluation now but given the logo here, we should re-qualify via BD- BANT and more, to progress this one 16/6: Prospect has no budget approval to run this project. 7/2/2025: Completed steps - Intro call, Solution overview with pre sales team, QC Call with Madhuri and Carl Next Steps: Schedule and Conduct a Demo Meeting: Action: Organize a demo meeting to present the Zycus solution to Bandhan Bank. High-level pricing estimates and the detailed feature comparison provided by Madhuri. Supporting documents and charts illustrating the benefits of a centralized, automated procurement process. Objective: Ensure clear, concise, and timely communication to reinforce the value proposition of our solution and prepare for the next phase of engagement.
hard.ais_why:
• Across 15+ months of dated rep notes covering RFP receipt, response, shortlisting and management presentation, there is zero buyer-side mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, autonomous, or any AI capability. The 17 Feb 2026 rep note records: 'Spoke to Mrinmoy Mahapatra from Bandhan Bank... told that we have filled the responses to the RFP shared by them... and clarified them to ask if they have any further queries about it.' The dialogue is entirely RFP-procedural, not AI-themed.
• On 9 Apr 2026 nishan.konar @bandhanbank.com confirmed: 'we have been shortlisted to top 6 vendors next round.' On 28 Apr 2026: 'We got the invitation from Dilip (Bandhan Bank) for presenting to their management around 10th May. Meeting will focus on Zycus differentiation and platform Demo.' Even the management pitch is framed as 'Zycus differentiation and platform Demo' ‚Äî no AI use cases, no Merlin demo focus, no agentic conversation despite Merlin being in product scope.
• 9 Avoma meetings with verified @bandhanbank.com attendees corroborate the engagement is real but generic. Confidence is High because the NextStep is unusually rich (4,381 chars across 15+ touchpoints) and the absence of any AI keyword across that volume is itself strong evidence. Merlin appears in Products but never in conversation; this is a traditional BFSI P2P RFP, not an AI-driven evaluation.

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, live deal ranked #1 of 7 on product and technology, but the 08 Jul close is impossible: the CFO/CIO final presentation is only being scheduled for the week of 13 Jul (after close), commercials are not yet exchanged, and top-3 negotiation plus purchase-committee approval still lie ahead. The date will slip to roughly Sep 2026; the deal itself is on a positive trajectory.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 10 Feb 2025, qualified 17 Feb 2026, close 08 Jul 2026 (2 days out) = ~513-day BFSI cycle. Shortlisted since ~02 Jul (down-select from 7 vendors). Remaining path from 02 Jul: CFO/CIO final presentation week of 13 Jul (AFTER close), commercial-template exchange (buyer-promised 23 Jun, not received), pricing and top-3 negotiation, purchase-committee approval by threshold, then MSA/InfoSec/contracting. Minimum realistic runway 8-10 weeks = Sep 2026. Pace required to hit 08 Jul is not achievable; forward slip ~60-75 days.
evidence: 02 Jul call, Nishan Kumar: 'it was probably the best presentation of all the 7 vendors... Product wise, you got number one. Even technology, you are number one. It is in that implementation where you got lower scores.'
02 Jul call, Nishan Kumar: 'we decided you will get another chance with our CFO... It will be probably 10 days from now.'
29 Jun Next_Step: 'good overall feedback and only negative points for no India BFSI implementation... 1. directly ask for commercials and negotiate with top3 vendors 2. CFO might call us for a demo.'
23 Jun Next_Step: 'he asked that we will get the details and commercial template over the email soon.'
16 Jun Next_Step: 'Prospect has no budget approval to run this project.'

metrics.narrative: The business problem is heavily manual, decentralized procure-to-pay across IT and non-IT procurement plus payments; Nishan Kumar framed the mandate on 17 Apr as 'maximum automation... minimize manual work... that is the north star of this product.' The only volume figure surfaced is non-IT spend of 'roughly around 64 per annum' (Nirmal, 17 Apr); no savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time, or ROI percentage has been quantified by the buyer. On 02 Jul Nishan warned the CFO will ask for 'ballpark figures on the costs of the whole thing', so a value case is now needed but not built. The move is to quantify automation savings, headcount optimization, and GRN/payment-status error reduction to anchor price for the CFO session.

economic_buyer.narrative: The final decision sits with the CFO and f&a head together with the CIO; Nishan Kumar stated on 17 Jun that 'your final evaluation will be done with the top management, our CFO... and the CIO', and on 02 Jul named the CFO as 'Rajiv' who 'keeps shuttling between Calcutta and Mumbai' (a champion-reported name, not a Salesforce contact, so not promoted as a confirmed stakeholder). Zycus has never met the CFO or CIO; first exposure is the final presentation being set up for the week of 13 Jul, which Carl Kimball (VP APJ) will lead. A budget-authority flag exists: the 16 Jun Next_Step records 'Prospect has no budget approval to run this project', so budget release is unconfirmed. Move: use the CFO session to convert the economic buyer from named to engaged and confirm funding.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Saipayan Dutta, Deputy Vice President (Decision Maker, primary)

decision_criteria.narrative: Nishan Kumar spelled out the exact scoring model on 02 Jul: 'there were 3 criteria, product, implementation, and technology... Product, you are number one. Even technology, you are number one. It is in that implementation where you got lower scores.' Functional criteria come from the 17 Apr requirements-gathering: maximum automation with minimal manual intervention, maker-checker controls on GRN, single-entry of data from RFP through to payment, AI/RPA-driven AP invoice reading, a modern user experience versus the legacy Java form-based ERP, catalog-based procurement, and native contract management with rate charts. The CFO/CIO gate adds implementation credibility, integration with Oracle, security and delegation-of-authority (DOP) as the decisive criteria (Nishan, 02 Jul).
sources: 17 Apr 2026 P2P Meeting (Anmol, Mrinmoy Mahapatra, Arun, Nirmal)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal 7-vendor RFP: techno-functional evaluation scored independently by technical and business committees, only qualified vendors invited to submit commercials, then multi-round negotiation with all qualified vendors, internal digital-note approval per delegation-of-authority, and a threshold-based purchase committee before PO/contract (Anmol, 17 Apr). The call sequence shows escalation: a full evaluation-team demo on 17 Jun (procurement, IT, payments, ex-Ronti Kar), a down-select to the top 3 (~02 Jul), and a final CFO/CIO management presentation being scheduled for the week of 13 Jul. Nishan confirmed the RFQ/commercials happen AFTER the CFO panel: 'RFQ thing will happen post our call with CFO and panel' (02 Jul).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are known at a high level: after purchase-committee approval by threshold, a PO/POA and contract are issued while the MSA signing runs in parallel, with vendor onboarding via the eGL/EGM master and a TPRM questionnaire (Anmol, 17 Apr). Third-party/supplier risk and MSA governance are handled separately by the vendor-management team on ServiceNow (Nirmal, 17 Apr). No India BFSI implementation partner or SOW has been formalized yet, and the InfoSec and Oracle-integration deep-dive is still ahead. Move: scope the delivery-partner arrangement and confirm the MSA and purchase-committee path in the CFO session.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and specific: 'the entire process is manual', managed over email and phone with data held in Excel (Anmol, 17 Apr); a legacy Java form-based ERP with poor user experience that Mrinmoy Mahapatra wants replaced with a modern web-like interface (17 Apr); no maker-checker on GRN so 'a user may wrongly cancel a GRN without understanding their financial impact' (Arun, 17 Apr); multi-point re-entry of data from RFP to vendor creation to payment; and vendors chasing payment status manually, which IT wants eliminated via a vendor portal (Mrinmoy, 17 Apr). Pain owners are Mrinmoy Mahapatra (IT), Nirmal (non-IT procurement) and the payments team (Arun/Dilip). Confirmed across the full evaluation team.
sources: 17 Apr 2026 P2P Meeting (Anmol, Mrinmoy Mahapatra, Arun)

champion.narrative: Nishan Kumar (DVP - Strategy & Program Management) is a strong, active champion with CFO access who orchestrates the deal internally. He proactively shares insider scoring intel (02 Jul: 'you are coming 1st on product assessment'), coaches Zycus on exactly how to pass the CFO gate (agenda, ballpark cost, the external-consultant fix, DOP and AI focus), arranged the full evaluation-team demo on 17 Jun, and is setting up the CFO/CIO final meeting. Last contact 02 Jul; no cooling signals. He is the reason Zycus was given a 'second chance' after the implementation red flag. The gap is single-threading: he is currently the only route to power, so building a second relationship into the CFO/CIO tier is the priority.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Nishan Kumar, DVP - Strategy & Program Management

competition.narrative: This is a 7-vendor RFP now down to a top 3, with Zycus scored #1 on both product and technology; Nishan Kumar called Zycus 'the surprise package' that beat expected big names like Deloitte (02 Jul). The named rivals on record are Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba. No single competitor is buyer-preferred; the real competitive threat is implementation credibility, where a shortlisted rival differentiated by bringing an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant onto its proposal (Nishan, 02 Jul). Wedge: Zycus wins on product, technology and Merlin AI automation, so the path is to neutralize the India BFSI implementation gap with a named local banking delivery partner before the CFO meeting, then defend price with a quantified ROI case in the top-3 negotiation.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is genuinely positive and buyer-led: a full evaluation-team demo on 17 Jun, a down-select from 7 vendors to the top 3 with Zycus ranked #1 on product and technology (~02 Jul), and a CFO/CIO final presentation now being scheduled for the week of 13 Jul. Stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted on 02 Jul and forecast lifted Pipeline to Best Case. The single open drag is the India BFSI implementation-credibility gap plus a 16 Jun no-budget-approval flag.

items.change: Down-selected from 7 vendors to the top 3, scored #1 on product and #1 on technology
Stage advanced Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted
Forecast category lifted Pipeline to Best Case
Full evaluation-team demo delivered in Kolkata to procurement, IT, payments and ex-CIO Ronti Kar
Zycus Value Proposition deck shared post-demo
Dilip Dafda emailed confirming shortlist status and next steps for the P2P project

competitive_position.summary: Seven-vendor BFSI RFP down to a top 3, with Zycus scored #1 on product and #1 on technology as of 02 Jul (champion Nishan Kumar) and named the 'surprise package' over expected big brands like Deloitte. No single rival is buyer-preferred; the strongest CURRENT threat is not a specific logo but the India BFSI implementation-credibility gap, which a shortlisted competitor is exploiting by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant (02 Jul). Among the SF-listed field, GEP Worldwide is the closest size/fit peer. How we win: bring a named India-banking delivery partner to the CFO meeting to close the only red flag, then defend price with a quantified ROI case in the top-3 negotiation.

competitors.source: SF Competitors__c; 7-vendor shortlist per 02 Jul call

competitors.quote: Everyone expected to do, like, Deloitte or those big shots, but you are the surprise package.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize source-to-pay depth and the unlimited-user commercial model the buyer already views favourably (02 Jul).

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with #1 product/technology scoring and native AI-powered AP automation.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast enterprise-grade contract management and banking references across APAC.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is scored #1 on product and technology and aligned to the buyer's automation, AP, maker-checker and contract-management requirements; the one exposed criterion is India BFSI implementation experience, the sole red flag from the 17 Jun panel.

items.deliverable: Arrange a named India-banking delivery/advisory consultant for the CFO meeting

your final evaluation will be done with the top management, our CFO... and the CIO.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for a #1-scored deal whose only real exposure is implementation credibility, an unengaged economic buyer, and an unbuilt value case.
flags: Neutralize the sole red flag: bring a named ex-India-bank (IDFC/Axis-calibre) delivery partner to the CFO meeting to prove local BFSI implementation.
Multi-thread beyond Nishan Kumar into the CFO, CIO and Mrinmoy Mahapatra so power access does not rest on one champion.
Build a quantified automation ROI case (savings, headcount, GRN/payment-error reduction) to justify price in the top-3 negotiation.

champion_strength.summary: Nishan Kumar (DVP - Strategy & Program Management) is a strong, active champion with CFO access. He supplies real-time scoring intel, coaches Zycus on the CFO agenda and the implementation fix, arranged the full evaluation-team demo, and secured the CFO/CIO final meeting. Last contact 02 Jul, no cooling signals. Risk is single-threading: he is currently the only route to power.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; 02 Jul, 23 Jun, 17 Jun Next_Step and calls

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin is in product scope and Carl positioned the Merlin agentic platform and intake on the 17 Apr call, and Nishan flagged AI as a 'big selling point' for the CFO; but AIS is Negative (3.0) and the buyer has never driven an AI-themed conversation across 15+ months, so the AI story is under-positioned relative to its potential.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 3.0 / Negative: across 15+ months of dated notes and 9 buyer calls there is no buyer-initiated AI/Merlin/agentic conversation; the evaluation is a traditional BFSI P2P RFP scored on product, technology and implementation. The buyer does value 'maximum automation' and Nishan advised keeping the AI part ready for the CFO, so appetite is latent rather than absent.

items.detail: India BFSI implementation-credibility gap is the sole red flag; a shortlisted rival is fielding an ex-IDFC-bank consultant. Needs a named local banking delivery partner at the CFO meeting.
16 Jun note records the prospect has no budget approval to run the project; funding release is unconfirmed and must be validated in the CFO session.
Economic buyer (CFO) and CIO not yet met; access is single-threaded through champion Nishan Kumar until the week-of-13-Jul presentation.
08 Jul close is not achievable: CFO presentation is week of 13 Jul, commercials and top-3 negotiation and purchase-committee approval still lie ahead. Realistic close ~Sep 2026.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and buyer-led: last activity 04 Jul, champion contact 02 Jul, a scheduled CFO meeting. No cooling. The forecast NUMBER is indefensible (Best Case on a 2-day close that will slip), but the deal itself is warming.
items: Buyer down-selected Zycus to the top 3 and ranked it #1 on product and technology (02 Jul).
Buyer proactively created a second CFO chance and is scheduling the final presentation (02 Jul).
Dilip Dafda emailed confirming shortlist status and next steps (04 Jul).

items.action: Line up a named ex-India-bank delivery partner to co-present at the CFO meeting.
items.trigger: Nishan: implementation was the only red flag; a rival fielded an ex-IDFC-bank consultant.
items.expected_effect: Closes the sole scoring gap before the decisive CFO session and protects the #1 ranking.

items.action: Build the ballpark commercial and a quantified automation ROI case for the CFO.
items.trigger: Nishan warned the CFO will ask ballpark cost; no value case is quantified.
items.expected_effect: Lets Zycus justify price to the economic buyer and enter top-3 negotiation from strength.

items.action: Have Carl Kimball lead the CFO/CIO final presentation and open the economic-buyer relationship.
items.trigger: First CFO/CIO exposure scheduled for week of 13 Jul; EB never met.
items.expected_effect: Converts the economic buyer from named to engaged and confirms budget release.

items.action: Re-engage Mrinmoy Mahapatra on Oracle integration and the vendor-portal payment-status ask.
items.trigger: IT integration is a CIO criterion; May outbound calls to Mrinmoy went unanswered.
items.expected_effect: Multi-threads to IT power and de-risks the technology gate before contracting.

items.action: Prepare the top-3 pricing envelope and negotiation strategy for the RFQ round.
items.trigger: Nishan: RFQ happens post the CFO panel; buyer negotiates with top 3.
items.expected_effect: Readies a defensible pricing structure to win the post-CFO commercial negotiation.

customer_preference.evidence: Nishan Kumar, 02 Jul: 'Product wise, you got number one. Even technology, you are number one'; 'probably the best presentation of all the 7 vendors... the surprise package.'

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain confirmed (fully manual P2P, no maker-checker on GRN, legacy Java ERP) plus one figure, non-IT spend 'roughly around 64 per annum' (Nirmal, 17 Apr); no ROI, savings, or headcount target quantified by the buyer.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is the #1-scored vendor of 7, down to the top 3, with a strong CFO-connected champion racing us toward a week-of-13-Jul CFO presentation; the only things between us and the win are proving India BFSI implementation credibility and engaging an economic buyer we have never met.

win_position.text: Earns ~72 and holds near the Shortlisted cap because Nishan Kumar confirmed Zycus scored #1 on both product and technology and beat expected names like Deloitte (02 Jul), the strongest possible buyer-preference signal.
Capped below that on one live risk: implementation credibility is the sole red flag, and a shortlisted rival is exploiting it by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant (Nishan, 02 Jul).
Further held back because the economic buyer (CFO/CIO) has never been met and the 16 Jun note flags 'no budget approval to run this project', so power and funding are unconfirmed.

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-led momentum is strong: down-select to the top 3, a full evaluation-team demo on 17 Jun, and a CFO/CIO final presentation the buyer is scheduling for the week of 13 Jul (Nishan, 02 Jul).
Seniority is rising and commercials are entering: the CFO, f&a head and CIO join next, and the buyer signalled RFQ/commercial negotiation with the top 3 immediately after (Nishan, 02 Jul).

customer_commitment.text: The buyer created a 'second chance' CFO session specifically to let Zycus defend the one gap, and Dilip Dafda emailed confirming shortlist status and next steps (04 Jul).
Commitment is not yet contractual: the promised commercial template (23 Jun) has not arrived and budget approval is unconfirmed.

deal_risk.text: The 08 Jul close is impossible and will slip ~60-75 days to roughly Sep 2026, since the CFO meeting alone is week of 13 Jul with negotiation and purchase-committee approval still ahead.
Access is single-threaded through Nishan Kumar; without a second relationship into the CFO/CIO tier the deal rests on one champion.

executive_alignment.evidence: CFO/CIO never met; access single-threaded via Nishan until week of 13 Jul.

partner_support.evidence: India BFSI implementation credibility is the sole red flag; a rival fields an ex-IDFC-bank consultant.

timeline.evidence: 08 Jul close not achievable; realistic close ~Sep 2026.

reasons.detail: On the 02 Jul call champion Nishan confirmed Zycus scored #1 on both product and technology and was the 'surprise package' over Deloitte, but the sole remaining blocker is Zycus's India BFSI implementation experience. A shortlisted competitor is actively exploiting this gap by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant into the evaluation. With the CFO/CIO final close the week of 13 Jul and Zycus yet to lock a named India-banking delivery partner, this single credibility flag could flip a deal we otherwise lead - forfeiting a $150K forecasted BFSI logo.
reasons.metric: #1 on product & tech, yet India FSI experience is the sole blocker (02 Jul)
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Tanmay whether we have locked a named India-banking delivery partner for the 13 Jul CFO meeting to neutralize the ex-IDFC consultant the rival is fielding.
reasons.summary: Rival out-credits us on India banking delivery via an ex-IDFC-bank implementation consultant
reasons.evidence: Competitive position (02 Jul): 'the strongest CURRENT threat is ... the India BFSI implementation-credibility gap, which a shortlisted competitor is exploiting by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant'; champion Nishan named the only blocker as 'the Zycus FSI experience in India'.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 70 is carried by momentum 73 vs stage-expected 56; preference strength +1.00 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 73 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 54 is anchored on the stage win (70), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 73, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: Zycus is the #1-scored vendor of 7, down to the top 3, with a strong CFO-connected champion racing us toward a week-of-13-Jul CFO presentation; the only things between us and the win are proving India BFSI implementation credibility and engaging an economic buyer we have never met.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Earns ~72 and holds near the Shortlisted cap because Nishan Kumar confirmed Zycus scored #1 on both product and technology and beat expected names like Deloitte (02 Jul), the strongest possible buyer-preference signal.
Capped below that on one live risk: implementation credibility is the sole red flag, and a shortlisted rival is exploiting it by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant (Nishan, 02 Jul).
Further held back because the economic buyer (CFO/CIO) has never been met and the 16 Jun note flags 'no budget approval to run this project', so power and funding are unconfirmed.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the underlying read to ~75 — but we cap confidence at 70 until the stage advances and sign-off lands. Read the gap as 'earned, but not yet banked.'

bullets.text: Buyer-led momentum is strong: down-select to the top 3, a full evaluation-team demo on 17 Jun, and a CFO/CIO final presentation the buyer is scheduling for the week of 13 Jul (Nishan, 02 Jul).
Seniority is rising and commercials are entering: the CFO, f&a head and CIO join next, and the buyer signalled RFQ/commercial negotiation with the top 3 immediately after (Nishan, 02 Jul).

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: The 08 Jul close is impossible and will slip ~60-75 days to roughly Sep 2026, since the CFO meeting alone is week of 13 Jul with negotiation and purchase-committee approval still ahead.
Access is single-threaded through Nishan Kumar; without a second relationship into the CFO/CIO tier the deal rests on one champion.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Seven-vendor BFSI RFP down to a top 3, with Zycus scored #1 on product and #1 on technology as of 02 Jul (champion Nishan Kumar) and named the 'surprise package' over expected big brands like Deloitte.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: The buyer created a 'second chance' CFO session specifically to let Zycus defend the one gap, and Dilip Dafda emailed confirming shortlist status and next steps (04 Jul).
Commitment is not yet contractual: the promised commercial template (23 Jun) has not arrived and budget approval is unconfirmed.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Line up a named ex-India-bank delivery partner to co-present at the CFO meeting. (by 12 Jul)
Build the ballpark commercial and a quantified automation ROI case for the CFO. (by 13 Jul)
Have Carl Kimball lead the CFO/CIO final presentation and open the economic-buyer relationship. (by 17 Jul)

calls_omitted.meeting: Bandhan Bank <> Zycus - Next stage update - quick briefing session (2026-04-10)
Bandhan Bank <> Zycus - P2P Proposal Queries discussion (2026-03-16, x2)

calls_omitted.reason: no transcript content available; 15+ months old, context only

gaps: Economic buyer (CFO/CIO) never met; CFO named only by the champion (transcript), not a Salesforce contact, so not promoted as a confirmed stakeholder.
No quantified value case (ROI/savings/headcount) stated by the buyer beyond one non-IT spend figure.
Budget approval unconfirmed per 16 Jun note.
